programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: More kinds of plants are crammed together in the tropical rainforests than anywhere else on Earth. The result is astonishing beauty and intense competition - a plant battleground. New filming techniques allow us to enter the plants’ world and see it from their perspective and on their timescale. From fast-growing trees to flowers that mimic dead animals, this is a journey into a magical world that operates on a different timescale to our own.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: The dark, a world of opportunity for mammals. More than two-thirds prefer the cover of the night, with some now returning to the safety of the shadows in order to avoid us.

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: In Senegal, west Africa, live a group of chimpanzees led by an alpha male named David. He has already been alpha for three years - a time when leaders here are usually overthrown. To make matters worse, David has no allies - no-one to help him defend his leadership. As the dry season sets in, the group are forced closer together to survive. But David is now surrounded by rivals who all want his crown and are prepared to kill him for it. David faces brutal battles, has his world engulfed in flames and has to pull off an extraordinary act of deception. In a story of power and politics, can David overcome the threats to his leadership and hold on to the alpha position long enough to sire a possible future heir to his throne?

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: A mother puma must battle rivals, tackle prey nearly three times her size and endure the wild mountain weather of Patagonia in her bid to raise four cubs – her biggest litter yet.

programme.title: UEFA Women's Champions League
programme.desc: Arsenal host OL Lyonnes in their Champions League semi-final second leg.

programme.title: Paddy & Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Paddy 'The Baddy' Pimblett and 'Meatball' Molly McCann, two of the biggest names in the world of UFC, head to Vegas as Paddy prepares to fight against Tony Ferguson in the Octagon.

programme.title: Paddy & Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Paddy is back in Liverpool to see Molly, who is now preparing for her own fight in Vegas on the back of two losses and feels her next fight could be the last of her career if she loses again.

programme.title: Paddy & Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: As pressure builds for her next fight, Molly visits a wedding venue with partner Ellis. Paddy discovers a serious shoulder injury which could scupper his next fight.

programme.title: Paddy & Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Paddy discusses his own mental health and the struggles and pressures he faces, whilst Molly trains in Liverpool for the last time before her fight in Vegas.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It's the first quarter-final, where this week’s six most talented amateurs gather to face off against each other for the first time.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: Alan Carr kicks off the competition by tasking ten interior designers to show off their signature styles by transforming a beach hut on the Dorset coast. They must also demonstrate their ability to reuse and repurpose where they can. Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Jonathan Adler must decide who has created the Stand Out Space and which designer will be the first to go home.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: When the FBI calls, Dr Ann Burgess joins a new task force hunting serial predators and is thrust into an active investigation involving the assault of over 20 women.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: The Piris plan a trip to Tippety Top to see the famous southern lights but lose each other in an ice fog. When Nugget thinks she sees a monster, Pancake proves to be an unlikely hero.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Big Lizard has found an epic crystal, and Cosima is jealous. Their squabbling reaches its peak at the top of the volcano! Will they learn to share before it's too late?

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: The freight train is here! Weasy wants to go shopping, but the ducklings want to play with their teddies. They secretly play while Weasy shops. Just when the train is about to leave, the ducklings realise that their teddy bears are still inside!

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: The heroes set out on a quest to find out who is draining colour from the forest and muster all their positivity to save it from greyness.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings turn into crab rescuers, but soon the house is full of them!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck gets a job in a cinema projection room, Frog manages to accidentally break the projector. Can Frog save the day and entertain the audience himself?

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: After a year apart, Roy visits Liko at Indigo Academy to ask for her help.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: Callum sets up the ultimate Grand Prix, and real F1 drivers give Joe advice on how to win - but the vehicles used are far from being cars! And the bros embark on a Mission Eggpossible.

programme.title: Gladiators: Epic Pranks
programme.desc: Nitro and Electro prank Fury at a fake wellness retreat! Prepare for fart whistles, bubble yoga, a bogus snail oracle and the slowest game of Unleash ever!

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Steve Backshall joins for a deadly quiz, His Majesty the King is awarded a Blue Peter Green Badge, Abby discovers some deadly flowers and fungi, and it’s the latest read from the Blue Peter Book Club.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads out to find one of the UK’s greatest predators: the dragonfly. He witnesses the aerial prowess of the hobby and dives underwater with the puffin.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is busy this week as the garden bursts into colour. Woody herbs such as thyme and sage don’t thrive in his clay soil, so he’s sowing seeds to ensure a steady supply. He’s planting grasses in the new Woodland Garden and creating a colourful pot for shade, and there's advice on what to do with this year’s daffodil bulbs. Carol Klein proves that small can be both beautiful and delicious, creating a modest border devoted to vegetables and weaving it together with flowers to provide year round colour. And Advolly Richmond heads to Fife to uncover the story of the first pink cupped daffodil, bred in the early 1900s and still growing today thanks to two passionate plantswomen. There’s a visit to a specialist nursery in Hertfordshire. With help from the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, they are preparing to exhibit trees fit for a changing climate at the RHS Chelsea Flower show. And it's all aboard a houseboat that's jam-packed with plants.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 15 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. Ainsley looks at homemade meals that you normally turn to when you are craving a takeaway. The menu includes fish and chips from James Martin, a pizza recipe from Keith Floyd, a curry from Lorraine Pascale, Nigel Slater's Chinese chicken thighs and the Hairy Bikers' take on a pork chow mein.

programme.title: UEFA Women's Champions League
programme.desc: Live coverage as Lyon take on Arsenal in the Women's Champions League.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Flog It! comes from the heart of Birmingham and its historic museum and art gallery. Antiques experts Christina Trevanion and Thomas Plant are searching the queue for the best antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Paul Martin explores the museum's world-class collection of pre-Raphaelite art and discovers the work of arts and crafts jewellery designers Arthur and Georgie Gaskin. Paul also visits Hagley Hall, the last great palladian house to be built in Britain.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: A rare medical condition meant Grace was unable to give birth, as she was born without a womb. But in a landmark moment for British medical history, her sister Amy made an extraordinary decision: to donate her womb to her sister in a complicated and involved operation, which lasted hours. Just months after the transplant, Grace became pregnant and now, with her husband, has a healthy daughter. It was a gift that transformed not only the sisters’ lives but those of everyone around them in a story of love, sacrifice and a deep family bond. Bill Bailey pairs Grace and Amy with Karen Turner, an award-winning artist with a powerful commitment to telling women’s stories. Karen works using an unusual and unforgiving technique, painting directly onto raw canvas. With no background layer and no possibility of correction, every paint stroke must be right. As Karen begins her work, she faces a profound challenge: how to capture the depth of love, trust and sacrifice that made this miracle possible. Can she find a way to express not just the medical achievement but the emotional truth behind it and create a portrait worthy of this remarkable act of sisterhood?

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Tough-talking Dom Littlewood and consumer champion Melinda Messenger turn their hand to antique dealing. Have Hampshire’s antique dealers met their match? In a break from hunting down cowboy builders and exposing wrongs, Dom and Melinda are in hot pursuit of profitable antiques. With £400 and a classic car, they’re paired with antiques experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Tim Medhurst.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 15 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: The Top of the Pops vaults are opened up to relive some of the classic and biggest songs of 1992. Featuring the hip-hop and dance of Kris Kross, SL2, Bizarre Inc and Felix to the rock stylings of Manic Street Preachers and Primal Scream, as well as the emergence of boy band royalty Take That, Miley’s dad Billy Ray getting the line dancing going, divas in the form of Annie Lennox and Mariah Carey - and, of course, Jimmy Nail. Plus many more.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: A look back at some of the songs performed on the show in 1993. Dance music and Euro pop continued to dominate the charts with performances from Haddaway, Sub Sub, Ace of Base and Robin S. The early stages of Britpop begin to emerge with Suede and Radiohead, and Icelandic superstar Björk makes an early appearance. Then there are the big sellers from Meat Loaf and Gabrielle, and the grooves come courtesy of Jamiroquai, Chaka Demus & Pliers and many more.

programme.title: Black Box
programme.desc: Obsessive forensic analyst Mathieu Vasseur is disappointed to be sidelined in the examination of the black box of an Atrian 800 airliner which has mysteriously crashed in the Alps. When his boss goes missing, Mathieu is asked to step in, and he concludes that there was an unauthorised entry into the cockpit. A press conference is held to announce the findings, but Mathieu is subsequently presented with conflicting evidence. French thriller with English subtitles.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: It’s ladies day at the races. Cat has to make a speech, and it forces her to confront what she has been denying about her relationship with Dylan. Ollie misbehaves. With alcohol and adrenaline in the air, it’s not long before rivalries are stirred up and best behaviour gives way to chaos. Shannon and Arran are caught in the crossfire as their families violently collide.

programme.title: Mint
programme.desc: As Dylan rages at Shannon in the wake of ladies day, she escapes and spends a life-changing day with Arran. Spending time together in a world of their own, they grow closer than ever. Meanwhile, tensions erupt between Cat and Dylan, making them question the foundations of their life together. Can they find a way to salvage what they have built? And will the family survive what comes next?

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: When Matty and Leanne catch each other stealing from their casino, they agree to stop. But as their lives spiral out of control, the pact they have made frays.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Take a Hike
programme.desc: This week, we’re in the Highlands and Islands of splendid Scotland. It’s home to five keen walkers, all hoping to walk off with the prize. Today, it’s 40-year-old Cathy’s turn to lead the walk. She moved to the Isle of Mull three years ago, where she works as a technical manager in a biscuit factory. She’s planned a coastal walk from the picture-postcard town of Tobermory (recognisable as the setting for the children’s series Balamory). As an adopted Scot, she’s opted for some traditional Scottish activities to entertain the group. Can Cathy wow the walkers with the charms of Mull and win all the points?

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild microclimate have long made it one of Britain’s most productive growing regions. Early spring brings the so-called hungry gap, but the vale has a long history of producing food, even in these leaner months. From the first spears of its world-famous asparagus to high-tech growers producing food year round, and from historic market gardens to traditional orchards alive with early pollinators, Adam and Sammi explore a landscape shaped by both tradition and innovation. While fresh produce may be scarce elsewhere, the Vale of Evesham is already beginning to flourish. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith investigates how falling milk prices are leading some dairy farmers to call it a day, despite record exports and booming milk production.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie visits a dairy farm near Torthorwald in Dumfriesshire at calving time. With 700 newborns due, he finds out about the benefits of block calving. Meanwhile, Shahbaz visits the seal colony in the Ythan Estuary in Aberdeenshire to find out how drones are being used for conservation, and Arlene is in Easter Ross to meet a woman who has been raising menopause awareness. Cammy is on home turf in Ayrshire as early potato planting gets under way, and as Landward continues to celebrate 50 years on air, Anne revisits a story from the archive that features cattle with a very unusual colour.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: There is lots of planting for the plate at Beechgrove Garden as Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham start growing a wide variety of edible produce. Kirsty is the first of the Beechgrove team to kick off work on her competition plot. Each presenter has been given a sense to appeal to in each of their competition borders, and Kirsty is planting things that will taste fantastic. She is also trying something new to Beechgrove: hops. Meanwhile, Brian provides an update on the veg he sowed at the start of the series and is in his rain garden checking on what survived this year's wet weather. There are all the usual handy hints, and there is a visit to Calum Clunie’s allotment in Leven.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Poppy O’Toole makes her prepares a brilliant weeknight supper of tikka salmon, spiced new potatoes and a cooling cucumber salad. Joe Hurd brings a taste of Sicily to the studio with stracchino-stuffed lamb chops with a side of the classic Italian caponata, all inspired by the late, great Antonio Carluccio. Ols Halas swaps the circus for the kitchen as he rustles up pork belly buns with poached rhubarb and dirty fries, while Jack Croft and Will Murray reveal the secrets to their most popular restaurant dish – confit cabbage with chestnuts, crispy cabbage leaves and vibrant wild garlic sauce. Chris Stark does work experience at the French Ambassador’s Residence for a crash course in fine dining. Greg Davies tucks into either his food heaven, beef, or his food hell, seafood! Meanwhile, Rick Stein gets his hands dirty digging for Cornish spuds, and Clive Myrie enjoys an aphrodisiac in the Dominican Republic.

programme.title: Andi Oliver’s Fabulous Feasts
programme.desc: Andi is in Bristol to help Jasper Thompson, a local man whose pioneering homeless project transforms shipping containers into temporary accommodation. He wants to raise awareness about his new community cafe, so who better than Andi to help him put on an unforgettable launch party with a Caribbean flavour? To entice locals to the event and kick off the cafe with a bang, Andi must put together a unique and delicious feast that draws on her and Jasper’s own roots in Antigua and Jamaica while also paying tribute to Bristol’s wider Caribbean heritage, using only the best local produce. The cafe itself needs a complete refurb, but can Jasper get it ready in time, and can Andi harness the Bristolians' unique creative spirit to make sure the event’s a roaring success?

programme.title: Grand Hotel
programme.desc: During an eventful few days, the luxurious Grand Hotel in Berlin hosts the intersecting lives of a diverse group of guests, including a Russian ballerina fearful that her career is waning, a penniless baron, a terminally ill accountant and a powerful Prussian businessman desperate to make a deal. Classic star-studded drama from MGM studios.

programme.title: Women's Super League
programme.desc: Coverage of the Women's Super League clash between Leicester City and Chelsea.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public bring their antiques to be viewed and valued.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Following six farming families in some of Scotland’s most remote locations. Emma and Ewan move to their forever farm on Bute, while cousins Donald and David prepare for a busy lambing season.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 16 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

During the First World War, two soldiers are ordered across no-man's-land and deep into enemy territory to deliver a message. If they fail, an entire battalion, under the mistaken belief the Germans are retreating, will be ordered over the top and into a deadly trap.

programme.title: Here We Are
programme.desc: Aharon has devoted his life to raising his son Uri, where they live together in a gentle routine away from the real world. But Uri is autistic and now as a young adult it might be time for him to live in a specialised home. But Aharon feels deep down that Uri is not ready for this separation, so on the way to the home, decides to run away with his son and they hit the road. A journey that has unexpected consequences for Aharon and Uri’s quiet life together. In Hebrew with English subtitles.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents an hour of topical debate.

programme.title: Half Man
programme.desc: 2018. It’s Niall’s wedding day as he follows his estranged ‘brother’ Ruben into a barn, away from all his guests. Here, in an explosion of violence, Ruben brutally and shockingly attacks him. Understanding why proves to be the driving question of this drama as we are catapulted back through the decades.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: A mother calls 999. Her 11-year-old daughter has returned from staying at her dad’s house, saying that a stranger came into her bedroom in the night and sexually assaulted her. A forensic team immediately heads to the crime scene, and the house is examined for potential forensic evidence the suspect may have left behind.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: There’s no time for horsing around as two teams canter through an antiques fair at Wetherby racecourse in West Yorkshire. In red, and saddling up for some fun, are sisters Sharon and Hayley, with their expert Charles Hanson. On the blue team, brothers Harley and Joe are paired with expert Philip Serrell. With one hour on the clock, the reds close in on an item that’s on their shopping list – an enamelled scent bottle – but they turn their noses up when they learn the best price. Over with the blues, Phil tries to spice things up with a silver nutmeg grater. Sadly, the three-figure price tag puts them off, so they stop to consider another important question – which sauce they prefer on their chips! Meanwhile, Danny Sebastian is dishing up a challenge for fairgoers, asking them to identify a mystery metal object. It’s silver-plated with a plunger at one end and a perforated acorn-shaped bowl at the other. What could it be?

programme.title: Secret Garden
programme.desc: David Attenborough reveals how tawny owls, badgers, dormice and bumblebees have made a home in a beautiful woodland garden hidden at the bottom of a steep-sided Welsh valley.

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Kelly Cates presents highlights of three Premier League matches. Manchester United face Liverpool at Old Trafford, with both teams fighting to secure Champions League football next season. Elsewhere, relegation-threatened Tottenham Hotspur travel to high-flying Aston Villa, whilst Bournemouth, another team hoping to secure European football next season, take on Crystal Palace.

programme.title: The Titfield Thunderbolt
programme.desc: Ealing comedy about a group of villagers and their battle to preserve the local railway line by running it themselves, an enterprise that goes well until they are sabotaged in a midnight raid by the rival bus company. Undaunted, they wheel out the Thunderbolt, an ancient exhibit in the village museum. Now all that remains is to put on a satisfactory run for the stickler of a government inspector.

programme.title: Whisky Galore!
programme.desc: Classic Ealing comedy about a whisky-laden ship that runs aground off the coast of the Outer Hebrides during the Second World War. The local islanders have depleted their supply of the amber nectar and are overjoyed at the thought of stocking up again. But it is Sunday, and the teetotallers object to making free with the unexpected cargo.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 17 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of classic recipes from some of the BBC's best-loved television chefs. These dishes will help when you are planning the perfect picnic. The menu includes Paul Hollywood's pork pies with quail eggs, a superb sandwich from James Martin and Ainsley's own mustard, bacon and onion tarts.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel invites members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. Surprise finds in Witney, Oxfordshire, include a pair of seascapes brought over from Holland in a suitcase 100 years ago which prove to have been a good investment. There is also an album of watercolours unearthed at a jumble sale and now worth at least £10,000, and a bracelet of gold eggs.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and his team of experts invite members of the public to bring their antiques to be viewed and valued, with an option to sell at auction.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Jordan Banjo, Sian Lloyd, Nick Pickard and Ellie White take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins his journey at the Demilitarised Zone, the heavily fortified border that has divided the Korean peninsula since the end of the Korean War. From the track of a long-abandoned railway near the Imjin River, he explores one of the most tense frontiers in the world and reflects on a conflict that ended in ceasefire but never peace. From the DMZ, Michael travels south by rail into Seoul, arriving in a vast, fast-moving capital that has risen from the ruins of war to become one of the world’s most advanced cities. Exploring palaces and the restored Cheonggyecheon stream, he uncovers how tradition and innovation coexist in everyday Korean life. At the BBC’s Seoul bureau, Michael meets the news editor broadcasting news into North Korea, before sharing street food with a North Korean defector who poignantly describes the personal cost of division. Michael’s first Korean leg ends at Kukkiwon, the World Taekwondo Headquarters, where he discovers how Korea’s national martial art reflects discipline, resilience and a hard-won modern identity.

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of day 17 of the World Snooker Championship at the Crucible Theatre, Sheffield.

programme.title: The Great Escaper
programme.desc: Based on the true story of Bernard Jordan, an 89-year-old veteran of the D-Day landings, who in 2014 sneaked out of his seaside care home where he lived with his wife, Rene, to board a cross-channel ferry and attend the 70th anniversary commemoration in Normandy.

programme.title: Mud
programme.desc: Two young lads join a spirited mystery man’s quest to reunite with his one true love, unaware of the forces and feelings that must be faced. Acclaimed Arkansas-set coming-of-age drama.

programme.title: Diwana
programme.desc: Pyarelal, a lovable orphan, is taken in by a Muslim lady. When he gets arrested for murder, no one believes it, but to everyone's shock he admits that he is guilty.

programme.title: Ek Hi Raasta
programme.desc: Amar and Malti, two orphans, lead a harmonious life with their son, Raja. However, Amar gets murdered and circumstances force Malti to marry Amar's boss, Prakash, which is not accepted by Raja

programme.title: Tyagi
programme.desc: Village-based Shankar makes his living as a farmer, living with his dad, and two brothers. He borrows money to fund his brothers education in the city. But once in the city they meet with the underworld and begin to carry on illegal activities.

programme.title: Ustaad
programme.desc: Shamsher Singh, a runaway criminal, pretends to love Asha and gets married to her. However, on the wedding night, Shamsher leaves Asha and never returns.

programme.title: Aadmi Aur Insaan
programme.desc: Jai and Munish's friendship is ruined when both of them fall in love with the same girl, Meena. Things take a turn when Munish finds out Jai was involved in the case of a faulty railway bridge.

programme.title: Choron Ki Baarat
programme.desc: Anju, a beautiful and spoilt woman, falls in love with a mysterious man, Shekhar. However, with time she realises that he has other motives.

programme.title: Khote Sikkay
programme.desc: An unknown horseback rider is after Jhanga, an evil and troublesome gangster who has terrorised everybody in the village, because he is responsible for his father's death.

programme.title: Dhund
programme.desc: After crashing his car on a foggy night, a man seeks help at a nearby home, where he encounters a dead man and a woman holding a gun. After hearing her sad story, he helps her hide the crime.

programme.title: Bandhan
programme.desc: A wastrel father and his righteous son have a showdown and the son is charged with his father's murder. Court-room drama follows as friends and family aren't sure whom to believe and testify for.

programme.title: Gair
programme.desc: An abandoned child raised by a wealthy household takes over the family business. However, he is resented by their natural son who, fueled by a bitter uncle, tries his best to make him lose face.

programme.desc: Bhawani Prasad, a criminal who poses as a holy man and is estranged from his wife and son, forcibly takes his grandson, Kundan, with him to follow in his footsteps.

programme.desc: Vishnu, a reputed judge, is assumed to be Anil, an absconding murderer. However, he decides to travel to Anil's home town and unravel the mystery behind his lookalike's existence.

programme.desc: Ajit, an orphan, loves his younger sister, Rajjo, and plans on getting her married into a respectable family. Things change when he tries to save her from a sexual assault.

programme.desc: A lawyer decides to defend a man who has been falsely accused of murder as he knows the real killer is his future father-in-law, who is also the judge presiding over the case.

programme.desc: Rajesh, a scientist, works with the Indian Government on a confidential research project. He is forced to deal with miscreants who wish to steal his research.

programme.desc: Vishwanath and Jaikishan are two thieves and good friends. When they try to lead an honest life for the sake of their women, Pukhraj turns them against each other and the two become sworn enemies.

programme.desc: Two brothers are separated after their mother is raped and commits suicide. One turns into a criminal and he unknowingly works for the same rapist, whereas the other becomes a policeman.

programme.desc: Peppa's older cousin Chloe comes to visit, and Peppa must play games by somebody else's rules for a change.

programme.title: Odo
programme.desc: Odo and Doodle volunteer to spend the day protecting a delicate plant. With the help of their friends Reggie the Fox and Michael the Mouse, they keep their leafy friend safe.

programme.title: Milkshake! World of Dance
programme.desc: Join Kiera as she discovers all about the wonderful world of dancing with the help of some Milkshakers. Kiera discovers all about the wonderful world of ballet dancing.

programme.title: Milkshake! Story Den
programme.desc: Join Olivia for a story about a day in late October, when Georgia notices objects moving around the house. Is it something spooky!? Clever Georgia debunks all the mysteries with explanations and no fear!

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: Start the day with a dose of hard-hitting debate, your phone calls and a little fun - all driven by Jeremy's unique style.

programme.title: Storm & Alexis
programme.desc: Two of 5's much-loved faces team up to focus on the issues that matter most to viewers - with your opinions front and centre.

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: National and international news.

programme.title: Matt Allwright
programme.desc: Matt looks at the big news stories developing during the day and offers help and advice to save you money and make your lives a little easier.

programme.title: Vanessa
programme.desc: Raw, unfiltered, authentic and personal, Vanessa Feltz wants you to tell her what's on your mind. Fiery discussions, a good giggle and plenty of afternoon gossip!

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: Castle and Beckett investigate the murder of an art gallery owner, but the lead suspect has lost his memory.

programme.title: Castle
programme.desc: When a bridesmaid fails to show on the wedding day... because she's dead, it falls to Castle and Beckett to find the murderer. And Castle meets an old girlfriend - the bride Kyra.

programme.title: Love Nature
programme.desc: In Holywood, County Down, a piece of land has been transformed into an edible forest garden shaped by patience, dedication, and care for wildlife.

programme.title: On Safari with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Jane discovers the majestic continent of Africa as a spectacular adventure awaits her on a once-in-a-lifetime safari in Kenya, where she also spends an unforgettable night sleeping under stars.

programme.title: Love Nature
programme.desc: In Eastbourne, we join locals who have planted thousands of trees hoping to make the town carbon neutral. Founded six years ago, the charity now has hundreds of volunteers rewilding.

programme.title: Susan Calman's Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Susan takes a trip to the South Downs home of Winnie the Pooh, enjoying picture postcard scenery and a dose of childhood nostalgia, topped off with a trip to Hundred Acre Wood.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Martain Anderson and fellow motorway cops pursue a BMW driving on cloned plates. What do the driver and passengers have to hide? And in Newcastle, PC Jamie Leslie follows a car doing 50mph in a 30 zone.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Firearms Squad responds to reports of a machete gang trying to break into a house. And a rude suspected drink driver is caught pulling racetrack moves with a baby on the back seat.

programme.title: Number One Fan
programme.desc: TV star Lucy Logan is saved from a mugging by Donna, who claims to be her number one fan. Donna is taken into Lucy's inner circle, but she has an ulterior motive...

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: All roads policing officers are called to attend a potentially fatal collision on the M5. PC Webb calls for back up when a man he attempts to handcuff pushes him into the path of the lorries on the road.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers pursue a speeding car when the driver fails to stop, before heading the wrong way down a dual carriageway. Near Derby, the race is on to save a driver whose car has careered into a swamp.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The firearms team gets locked into a stand-off against potential drug dealers. Steve deals with two teens, one who crashed a car and one who got into a fight.

programme.title: Honeymoon
programme.desc: A newlywed couple's idyllic country-lake honeymoon descends into chaos.

programme.title: Agnes
programme.desc: A nun’s disturbing behavior sparks rumors of demonic possession at a remote convent. Benjamin, a priest-in-waiting, and his disillusioned mentor, Father Donaghue, are sent to investigate, their methods backfire, leaving a wake of terror and trauma.

programme.title: Censor
programme.desc: Film censor Enid (Niamh Algar, Wrath of Man) is assigned to review a disturbing film that recalls her childhood memories. She begins to unravel how this eerie work might be tied to the long-ago disappearance of her sister.

programme.title: Playback
programme.desc: A group of high school students digging into their town's dark past unearth a chilling secret, releasing an evil spirit that possesses its victims in this frightening thriller.

programme.title: Flawless
programme.desc: An aging janitor and an American executive who form an unlikely alliance in order to carry out a jewel heist.

programme.title: Headhunters (English dub)
programme.desc: An accomplished headhunter risks everything to obtain a valuable painting owned by a former mercenary.

programme.title: Sushi Girl
programme.desc: Fish is released from jail and the men he protected celebrate with dinner.

programme.title: The Protector 2 (English dub)
programme.desc: KHAM (Tony Jaa) is forced to run as he fights to clear his name. He is hunted by not only the police, but Boss Suchart’s revengeful twin nieces and LC (RZA), a crime lord with his own agenda.

programme.title: Fay Grim
programme.desc: A single mother from Queens becomes unwittingly embroiled in international espionage.

programme.title: Grand Piano
programme.desc: Tom Selznick (Elijah Wood) is the most talented pianist of his generation, but has stopped performing in public because of his stage fright.

programme.title: Ragnarok (English dub)
programme.desc: Sigurd is intrigued by an inscription and believes this is the key to the myth of Ragnarok.

programme.title: The Hunter
programme.desc: Willem Dafoe plays a mercenary sent by a corporation to the untamed wilderness of Tasmania to track down a tiger thought to be extinct, whose genetic code holds the secret to a dangerous weapon.

programme.title: Frontera
programme.desc: Miguel crosses the Arizona-Mexican border illegally and is accused of murdering the wife of a former sheriff. His pregnant wife gets involved with Mexican smugglers trying to help him.

programme.title: Blackthorn
programme.desc: Sam Shepard is Butch Cassidy in this western about the legendary outlaw's return from exile.

programme.title: Body at Brighton Rock
programme.desc: A novice park ranger must spend the night guarding a potential crime scene.

programme.title: Compliance
programme.desc: Inspired by true events, Compliance tells the chilling story of just how far one might go to obey a figure of authority.

programme.title: Satanic
programme.desc: 4 friends seek dark adventure and end up in a demonic time loop.

programme.title: Donkey Punch
programme.desc: Seven young adults party aboard a luxury yacht, but when one of them dies, it's a ruthless fight for survival.

programme.title: The Perfect Host
programme.desc: Warwick is the consummate host. John is a career criminal. We discover just how deceiving appearances can be.

programme.title: Borderline
programme.desc: A helplessly romantic sociopath escapes from a mental institution and invades the home of a ‘90s pop superstar. He just wants to be loved; she just wants to survive.

programme.title: The Ones Below
programme.desc: A young affluent couple expecting their first child hits it off with the new couple that moves in downstairs, until a dinner party between them ends in a shocking accident.

programme.title: Amulet
programme.desc: Tomaz, an ex-soldier is living in a decaying house, inhabited by a young woman and her dying mother. He starts to fall for his new companion but cannot ignore his suspicion that something insidious might also be living alongside them.

programme.title: Splinter
programme.desc: Trapped in a gas station by a parasite, a couple and an escaped convict must find a way to survive.

programme.title: Aniara
programme.desc: The human race must leave Earth and begin new on Mars. After takeoff the ship Aniara is damaged beyond repair. As they cope with their new reality passengers begin to lose their sanity.

programme.title: Beyond the Black Rainbow
programme.desc: Held captive in a specialized medical facility, a young woman seeks a chance to escape her obsessed captor.

programme.title: Held
programme.desc: A married couple is held hostage by an unseen, menacing stranger. The situation becomes brutal and they must work together to uncover the truth and find a way out before it‚Äôs too late.

programme.title: The Good Doctor
programme.desc: Martin Blake (Orlando Bloom) is an ambitious but anxious young doctor, eager to impress his superiors and colleagues.

programme.title: Night Catches Us
programme.desc: An ex-black panther returns home to find himself right back into the rivalries and love affair he left behind.

programme.title: The Job
programme.desc: Bubba (Patrick Flueger) gets a tip from a drifter (Ron Perlman) about a job offer, Bubba's girl Joy is thrilled. Bubba will have to learn if he has the killer instincts to do what it requires.

programme.title: The Two Faces of January
programme.desc: This thriller follows Chester MacFarland (Viggo Mortensen) and his wife Colette (Kirsten Dunst) as they befriend a guide (Oscar Isaac) on a European trip. This friendship takes a turn when it morphs into a love triangle full of envy, and murder.

programme.title: Shuttle
programme.desc: When two friends return from a girls' weekend vacation in Mexico, they find themselves stranded at the airport.

programme.title: The Eclipse
programme.desc: Michael Farr (Ciaran Hinds), a teacher raising his two kids alone since his wife died two years earlier, has been seeing and hearing strange things late at night.

programme.title: Marrowbone
programme.desc: Four young siblings vow to stay together when their mother dies. But when a ghostly presence torments them and a lawyer threatens their pact, the four must stand together or be torn apart forever.

programme.title: The Evil Next Door
programme.desc: Shirin moves into a new home with her partner, and his son Lucas. Shirin realizes that they are haunted by an evil that wants Lucas at any cost.

programme.title: Apartment 143
programme.desc: A team of parapsychologists sets out to investigate a series of anomalous phenomena taking place in a newly occupied apartment.

programme.title: Good Neighbors
programme.desc: Neighbors Spencer (Scott Speedman) and Louise (Emily Hampshire) have bonded over their fascination with a recent string of murders terrorizing their community.

programme.title: Detour
programme.desc: Harper (Tye Sheridan) suspects his stepdad Vincent (Stephen Moyer) of causing the car crash his mother was in. Johnny Ray (Emory Cohen) offers to “take care” of Vincent. Harper agrees. Afterwards, Harper protests but realizes there is no way out.

programme.title: Indemnity
programme.desc: An ex-firefighter in Cape Town wakes up next to his murdered wife, with no recollection of what transpired. On the run from both the police and a mysterious third party, he must fight for his life and find out who killed his wife.

programme.title: Bronson
programme.desc: In 1974, a misguided 19 year old named Michael Peterson decided he wanted to make a name for himself and so, he attempted to rob a post office.

programme.title: Riders of Justice
programme.desc: Recently deployed Markus returns home to care for his daughter after his wife is killed in an accident. But, Markus begins to suspect his wife was murdered and embarks on a mission of revenge.

programme.title: The Last King (English dub)
programme.desc: Set in the Middle Ages, in the midst of a great civil war. Warriors risk their life to shepherd an infant heir across snowy mountains to claim his rightful place on the throne.

programme.title: Mickey 17
programme.desc: A disposable employee is sent on a human expedition to colonize the ice world Niflheim. After one iteration dies, a new body is regenerated with most of his memories intact.

programme.title: Glass
programme.desc: David Dunn tries to stay one step ahead of the law while delivering vigilante justice on the streets of Philadelphia. His special talents soon put him on a collision course with the Beast -- the psychotic madman who has superhuman strength and 23 distinct personalities. Their epic showdown leads them to an encounter with the mysterious Elijah Price, the criminal mastermind who holds critical secrets for both men.

programme.title: Split
programme.desc: Though Kevin has evidenced 23 personalities to his trusted psychiatrist, Dr. Fletcher, there remains one still submerged who is set to materialize and dominate all the others. Compelled to abduct three teenage girls led by the willful, observant Casey, Kevin reaches a war for survival among all of those contained within him -- as well as everyone around him -- as the walls between his compartments shatter.

programme.title: Superman II
programme.desc: Superman (Christopher Reeve) foils the plot of terrorists by hurtling their nuclear device into outer space, but the bomb's shock waves free the Kryptonian villain General Zod (Terence Stamp) and his henchmen Ursa (Sarah Douglas) and Non (Jack O'Halloran) from their imprisonment. Traveling to Earth, they threaten the planet with destruction at the same time that Superman decides to renounce his superpowers in order to live a normal life as Clark Kent with his new love, Lois Lane (Margot Kidder).

programme.title: Superman III
programme.desc: Computer programmer Gus Gorman (Richard Pryor) is hired by financial tycoon Ross Webster (Robert Vaughn) to seize control of a weather satellite and annihilate Colombia's coffee crop. When Superman (Christopher Reeve) manages to thwart the plan, Webster commands Gorman to use the satellite to locate kryptonite, the Man of Steel's mortal weakness. But a missing unknown element in the kryptonite -- replaced by Gorman with tar -- causes an unintended side effect when presented to Superman.

programme.title: Nico, 1988
programme.desc: Approaching age 50, singer/songwriter Nico leads a solitary existence -- far from her days as a Warhol superstar and celebrated vocalist for the Velvet Underground in the 1960s. Her life and career on the fringes, Nico's new manager convinces her to hit the road again and tour in Europe to promote her latest album. Struggling with demons and the consequences of a muddled life, she longs to rebuild a relationship with her son, whose custody she lost long ago.

After a catastrophic crash on an unknown planet, pilot Mills quickly discovers he's actually stranded on Earth -- 65 million years ago. Now, with only one chance at a rescue, Mills and the only other survivor, Koa, must make their way across an unknown terrain riddled with dangerous prehistoric creatures.

programme.title: Sing Sing
programme.desc: A man is imprisoned at Sing Sing for a crime he didn't commit. When joined by a wary outsider, he finds purpose in staging an original comedy with a theater group of other incarcerated men.

programme.title: Miss You Already
programme.desc: A woman (Toni Collette) undergoes treatment to battle breast cancer while her best friend (Drew Barrymore) tries to get pregnant.

programme.title: They All Laughed
programme.desc: The employees of New York City's Odyssey Detective Agency can't seem to prevent themselves from getting over-involved in their clients' lives. When aging gumshoe John Russo (Ben Gazzara) is assigned to trail an Italian millionaire's wife, Angela (Audrey Hepburn), he inevitably falls for her continental charm. His partner, Charles (John Ritter), is similarly unable to keep his hands off the woman he's trailing -- a winsome cheating spouse named Dolores (Dorothy Stratten).

programme.title: Lady Bird
programme.desc: A teenager (Saoirse Ronan) navigates a loving but turbulent relationship with her strong-willed mother (Laurie Metcalf) over the course of an eventful and poignant senior year of high school.

programme.title: Love, Simon
programme.desc: Everyone deserves a great love story, but for 17-year-old Simon Spier, it's a little more complicated. He hasn't told his family or friends that he's gay, and he doesn't know the identity of the anonymous classmate that he's fallen for online. Resolving both issues proves hilarious, terrifying and life-changing.

programme.title: Won't Back Down
programme.desc: Jamie Fitzpatrick and Nona Alberts are two women from opposite sides of the social and economic track, but they have one thing in common: a mission to fix their community's broken school and ensure a bright future for their children. The two women refuse to let any obstacles stand in their way as they battle a bureaucracy that's hopelessly mired in traditional thinking, and they seek to re-energize a faculty that has lost its passion for teaching.

programme.title: Wonka
programme.desc: Armed with nothing but a hatful of dreams, young chocolatier Willy Wonka manages to change the world, one delectable bite at a time.

programme.title: Blue Beetle
programme.desc: Jaime Reyes suddenly finds himself in possession of an ancient relic of alien biotechnology called the Scarab. When the Scarab chooses Jaime to be its symbiotic host, he's bestowed with an incredible suit of armor that's capable of extraordinary and unpredictable powers, forever changing his destiny as he becomes the superhero Blue Beetle.

programme.title: Companion
programme.desc: A weekend getaway at a remote cabin turns to chaos when it's revealed that one of the guests -- a subservient android built for human companionship -- has gone haywire.

programme.title: Get Out
programme.desc: Now that Chris and his girlfriend, Rose, have reached the meet-the-parents milestone of dating, she invites him for a weekend getaway with Missy and Dean. At first, Chris reads the family's overly accommodating behavior as nervous attempts to deal with their daughter's interracial relationship, but as the weekend progresses, a series of increasingly disturbing discoveries leads him to a truth that he never could have imagined.

programme.title: Our Kind of Traitor
programme.desc: A money launderer (Stellan Skarsgard) for Russian gangsters asks a couple vacationing in Marrakech, Morocco, to deliver incriminating evidence to an MI6 agent (Damian Lewis).

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts: A Natural History
programme.desc: Delving into the mythological, including dragons, mermaids, and unicorns -- alongside examinations of natural world beasts, such as vervet monkeys, narwhals, and manatees; Stephen Fry travels far and wide to learn about such animals.

programme.title: Parental Guidance
programme.desc: Minor-league baseball announcer Artie Decker (Billy Crystal) and his wife, Diane (Bette Midler), get to see their only child, Alice (Marisa Tomei), very seldom. To their surprise, Alice and her husband (Tom Everett Scott) ask them to watch their children so they can embark on a trip for work. It promises to be a fun time for all, until the three young ones turn out to be quite a handful for this old-fashioned couple. Artie and Diane may need to bend the rules to make everyone happy in the end.

programme.title: Despicable Me
programme.desc: Supervillain Gru, a man who delights in all things wicked, hatches a plan to steal the moon. Surrounded by an army of little yellow minions and his impenetrable arsenal of weapons and war machines, Gru prepares to vanquish all who stand in his way. However, nothing in his calculations and groundwork has prepared him for his greatest challenge: three adorable orphan girls who want to make him their dad.

programme.title: Dim Sum Funeral
programme.desc: In the wake of her death, the embittered children of Chinese-American matriarch Mrs. Xiao (Lisa Lu) begrudgingly return to their childhood home for a bristly -- and brief, they hope -- reunion. But after Xiao's maid and sole confidant, Viola (Talia Shire), reveals her wish to undergo a week-long Buddhist funeral rite, the younger Xiaos -- including plastic surgeon Alexander (Russell Wong) and lesbian actress Meimei (Steph Song) -- have seven days to come to terms with her death, and each other.

programme.title: The Best Exotic Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Some British retirees (Judi Dench, Maggie Smith, Bill Nighy) decide to outsource their retirement to exotic -- and less expensive -- India. Lured by advertisements for the newly restored Marigold Hotel and imagining a life of leisure in lush surroundings, they arrive and find that the Marigold is actually a shell of its former self. Though their new home is not quite what they had imagined, the retirees find that life and love can begin again when they let go of their pasts.

programme.title: Megan Leavey
programme.desc: The true life story of Megan Leavey, a young Marine corporal whose unique discipline and bond with a military combat dog saved many lives during her deployment in Iraq. Assigned to clean up the K-9 unit after a disciplinary hearing, Leavey starts to identify with Rex, a particularly aggressive dog that she trains. Over the course of their service, Megan and Rex complete more than 100 missions until an improvised explosive device injures both, putting their fates in jeopardy.

programme.title: Monster-in-Law
programme.desc: Charlotte (Jennifer Lopez) is smitten when she meets Dr. Kevin Fields (Michael Vartan). So when Kevin pops the question after they start dating, Charlotte happily accepts. But she soon realizes that Kevin's mom, Viola (Jane Fonda), is not quite thrilled to have a new family member. Viola, a newscaster, has just lost her job and is feeling rather attached to Kevin, so she regards Charlotte as her new competition -- and will apparently do anything to make her son call off the wedding.

programme.title: Bad Santa
programme.desc: In this dark comedy, the crotchety Willie T. Stokes (Billy Bob Thornton) and his partner (Tony Cox) reunite once a year for a holiday con. Posing as a mall Santa and his elf, they rip off shopping outlets on Christmas Eve. This year, however, Willie is falling apart. He's depressed and alcoholic, and his erratic behavior draws the suspicion of mall security (Bernie Mac). But when befriending a small boy brings out his kinder side, Willie begins to wonder if there is still some hope for him.

programme.title: Bad Santa 2
programme.desc: Fueled by cheap whiskey, greed and hatred, Willie Soke (Billy Bob Thornton) teams up with his angry little sidekick, Marcus, to knock off a Chicago charity on Christmas Eve. Along for the ride is chubby and cheery Thurman Merman, a 250-pound ray of sunshine who brings out Willie's sliver of humanity. Mommy issues arise when the pair are joined by Willie's horror story of a mother, Sunny Soke, who raises the bar for the gang's ambitions, while somehow lowering the standards of criminal behavior.

programme.title: Valentine's Day
programme.desc: In a series of interconnected stories, various Los Angeles residents (Jessica Alba, Jessica Biel, Bradley Cooper) wend their way through the highs and lows of love during a single day. As the holiday unfolds, they experience first dates, longtime commitments, youthful crushes and connections to old flames.

programme.title: Pitch Perfect 3
programme.desc: After the highs of winning the world championships, the Bellas find themselves split apart and discovering there aren't job prospects for making music with your mouth. But when they get the chance to reunite for an overseas USO tour, the group of nerds comes together to make some music one last time.

programme.title: Kong: Skull Island
programme.desc: Scientists, soldiers and adventurers unite to explore a mythical, uncharted island in the Pacific Ocean. Cut off from everything they know, they venture into the domain of the mighty Kong, igniting the ultimate battle between man and nature. As their mission of discovery soon becomes one of survival, they must fight to escape from a primal world where humanity does not belong.

programme.title: Happy Death Day
programme.desc: Tree Gelbman is a blissfully self-centered collegian who wakes up on her birthday in the bed of a student named Carter. As the morning goes on, Tree gets the eerie feeling that she's experienced the events of this day before. When a masked killer suddenly takes her life in a brutal attack, she once again magically wakes up in Carter's dorm room unharmed. Now, the frightened young woman must relive the same day over and over until she figures out who murdered her.

programme.title: The House
programme.desc: Scott and Kate Johansen must figure out a way to earn some money after their daughter's scholarship falls through. When all else fails, the desperate couple join forces with their neighbor Frank to start an underground casino in his home. As the cash rolls in and the good times fly, Scott and Kate soon learn that they may have bitten off more than they can chew.

programme.title: Missing
programme.desc: When the disappearance of a local girl overshadows the abduction of another, a mother's search for the truth behind her daughter's disappearance uncovers a web of jealousy, deception and corruption in a small town.

programme.title: Kingsman: The Golden Circle
programme.desc: With their headquarters destroyed and the world held hostage, members of Kingsman find new allies when they discover a spy organization in the United States known as Statesman. In an adventure that tests their strength and wits, the elite secret agents from both sides of the pond band together to battle a ruthless enemy and save the day, something that's becoming a bit of a habit for Eggsy.

programme.title: Patti Cake$
programme.desc: Fed up with life in her New Jersey hometown, Patricia Dombrowski, aka Patti Cake$, hopes to follow in the footsteps of her idol and hit the road to achieve stardom as a rapper. With help from her best friend, a mysterious musician and her loving grandmother, Patti leads the charge against an army of haters, unpaid bills and the broken dreams that are holding her back.

programme.title: Look Into My Eyes
programme.desc: A group of New York City psychics conducts deeply intimate readings for their clients, revealing a kaleidoscope of loneliness, connection and healing.

programme.title: Alex Cross
programme.desc: Alex Cross (Tyler Perry) works as a detective/psychologist for the Detroit Police Department along with his lifelong friend, Tommy Kane (Edward Burns), and detective Monica Ashe (Rachel Nichols). Cross' latest case puts him on a collision course with Picasso (Matthew Fox), a cunning serial killer whose ultimate target is multinational industrialist Giles Mercier (Jean Reno). Cross must find a way inside Picasso's head not only to catch the killer but also to stem the violent urges Cross feels.

programme.title: A Different Man
programme.desc: Aspiring actor Edward undergoes a radical medical procedure to drastically transform his appearance. But his new dream face turns into a nightmare as he becomes obsessed with reclaiming what he lost.

programme.title: Sucker Punch
programme.desc: Locked away, a young woman named Babydoll retreats to a fantasy world where she is free to go wherever her mind takes her. Determined to fight for real freedom, she finds four women -- Rocket, Blondie, Amber and Sweet Pea -- to join together to escape the terrible fate that awaits them. With a virtual arsenal at their disposal, the allies battle everything from samurais to serpents, while trying to decide what price they will pay for survival.

programme.title: Red Riding Hood
programme.desc: Young Valerie (Amanda Seyfried) feels torn between two men. She loves Peter, but her parents intend that she marry wealthy Henry. Valerie and Peter are going to run away together, but her sister is killed by a werewolf before they can put their plan into action. The villagers call on the help of Father Solomon (Gary Oldman), a famous monster hunter who warns that the beast takes human form by day. Then Valerie suspects that the werewolf is someone she loves.

programme.title: The Nun
programme.desc: When a young nun at a cloistered abbey in Romania takes her own life, a priest with a haunted past and a novitiate on the threshold of her final vows are sent by the Vatican to investigate. Together, they uncover the order's unholy secret. Risking not only their lives but their faith and their very souls, they confront a malevolent force in the form of a demonic nun.

programme.title: The Nun II
programme.desc: An evil spreads in 1956 France as a priest is murdered and Sister Irene once again comes face-to-face with the demonic nun Valak.

programme.title: House of 1000 Corpses
programme.desc: An empty fuel tank and a flat tire lead two couples down a terror-riddled road to the House of 1000 Corpses. House of 1000 Corpses is at its core a story of family - a cast of twisted individuals who, with each slash of a throat or stab thru the chest, add bodies to their sick human menagerie.

programme.title: Oracle
programme.desc: A young woman accepts a job on a property with a traumatic past. Upon her arrival, she soon realizes that the nightmares of her childhood are connected to the evil in the house.

programme.title: The Devil's Rejects
programme.desc: After a raid on the rural home of the psychopathic Firefly family, two members of the clan, Otis (Bill Moseley) and Baby (Sheri Moon Zombie), manage to flee the scene. Heading to a remote desert motel, the killers reunite with Baby's father, Capt. Spaulding (Sid Haig), who is equally demented and intent on maintaining their murder spree. While the trio continues to torment and kill various victims, the vengeful Sheriff Wydell (William Forsythe) slowly closes in on them.

programme.title: Stonewall
programme.desc: Kicked out by his parents, a gay teenager (Jeremy Irvine) leaves small-town Indiana for New York's Greenwich Village, where growing discrimination against the LGBT community leads to riots on June 28, 1969.

programme.title: Time Bandits
programme.desc: Young history buff Kevin (Craig Warnock) can scarcely believe it when six dwarfs emerge from his closet one night. Former employees of the Supreme Being (Ralph Richardson), they've purloined a map charting all the holes in the fabric of time and are using it to steal treasures from different historical eras. Taking Kevin with them, they variously drop in on Napoleon (Ian Holm), Robin Hood (John Cleese) and King Agamemnon (Sean Connery) before the Supreme Being catches up with them.

programme.title: The Daytrippers
programme.desc: Eliza D'Amico's (Hope Davis) seemingly idyllic marriage to Louis (Stanley Tucci) changes dramatically when she discovers what she believes to be a love letter to him from someone else. Going home to her family for comfort, Eliza sets out with her mother (Anne Meara), father (Pat McNamara), sister (Parker Posey) and her sister's boyfriend (Liev Schreiber) in the family car to track down and confront Louis. Always one step behind him, the family learns a lot about each other in the process.

programme.title: Fantastic Planet
programme.desc: This animated tale follows the relationship between the small human-like Oms and their much larger blue-skinned oppressors, the Draags, who rule the planet of Ygam. While the Draags have long kept Oms as illiterate pets, this hierarchy shifts after an Om boy becomes educated, thanks to a young female Draag. This leads to an Om rebellion, which weakens the Draag control over their race. Will the Oms and the Draags find a way to coexist? Or will they destroy each other?

programme.title: Pawn
programme.desc: A robbery turns into a tense hostage situation when three gunmen hold up a diner the mob uses as a front.

programme.title: The Flash
programme.desc: Worlds collide when the Flash uses his superpowers to travel back in time to change the events of the past. However, when his attempt to save his family inadvertently alters the future, he becomes trapped in a reality in which General Zod has returned, threatening annihilation. With no other superheroes to turn to, the Flash looks to coax a very different Batman out of retirement and rescue an imprisoned Kryptonian -- albeit not the one he's looking for.

programme.title: Cleaner
programme.desc: Hoping to expose corruption, radical activists take 300 hostages at an energy company's annual gala in a high-rise building. Their cause soon gets hijacked by an extremist within their ranks who's ready to kill everyone. It's now up to a former soldier who works as a window cleaner to save the hostages trapped inside, including her younger brother.

programme.title: Trap
programme.desc: A man and his teenage daughter realize they're at the center of a dark and sinister event while watching a concert.

programme.title: Knox Goes Away
programme.desc: Diagnosed with a rapidly evolving form of dementia, a contract killer gets the chance to redeem himself by saving the life of his estranged son.

programme.title: Mickey 17
programme.desc: A disposable employee is sent on a human expedition to colonize the ice world Niflheim. After one iteration dies, a new body is regenerated with most of his memories intact.

programme.title: Papillon
programme.desc: The epic story of Henri Papillon Charrière, a safecracker from the Parisian underworld who is framed for murder and condemned to life in the notorious penal colony on Devil's Island. Determined to regain his freedom, Papillon forms an unlikely alliance with convicted counterfeiter Louis Dega, who in exchange for protection, agrees to finance Papillon's escape.

programme.title: Trap
programme.desc: A man and his teenage daughter realize they're at the center of a dark and sinister event while watching a concert.

programme.title: A Nightmare on Elm Street
programme.desc: Teenagers Nancy, Quentin, Kris, Jesse and Dean are all neighborhood friends who begin having the same dream of a horribly disfigured man who wears a tattered sweater and a glove made of knives. The man, Freddy Krueger (Jackie Earle Haley), terrorizes them in their dreams, and the only escape is to wake up. But when one of their number dies violently, the friends realize that what happens in the dream world is real, and the only way to stay alive is to stay awake.

programme.title: It
programme.desc: In 1960, seven preteen outcasts fight an evil demon that poses as a child-killing clown. Thirty years later, they reunite to stop the demon once and for all when it returns to their hometown.

programme.title: The Hole in the Ground
programme.desc: One night, Sarah's young son disappears into the woods behind their rural home. When he returns, he looks the same, but his behavior grows increasingly disturbing. Sarah begins to believe that the boy who returned may not be her son at all.

programme.title: Halloween: The Curse of Michael Myers
programme.desc: This installment marks the return of the seemingly indestructible masked murderer Michael Myers (George P. Wilbur), who is targeting Tommy Doyle (Paul Stephen Rudd), a young man tied into the legacy of the killer and his connections with the Strode family. As the supernatural elements of Michael's macabre abilities are explored, his longtime adversary, Dr. Sam Loomis (Donald Pleasence), is also back in yet another attempt to stop the psychopath's brutal rampages.

programme.title: Halloween H20: 20 Years Later
programme.desc: Two decades after surviving a massacre on October 31, 1978, former babysitter Laurie Strode finds herself hunted by persistent knife-wielder Michael Myers. Laurie now lives in Northern California under an assumed name, where she works as the headmistress of a private school. But it's not far enough to escape Myers, who soon discovers her whereabouts. As Halloween descends upon Laurie's peaceful community, a feeling of dread weighs upon her -- with good reason.

programme.title: Halloween: Resurrection
programme.desc: He's back for more... When a group of teens win a contest to spend a night in Michael Myers' childhood home to be broadcast live on the Internet, they believe they are in for a little fun and some free publicity. But things go frightfully wrong and the game turns into a struggle to make it out of the house alive.

programme.title: Maze Runner: The Death Cure
programme.desc: Thomas leads some escaped Gladers on their final and most dangerous mission yet. To save their friends, they must break into the legendary Last City, a WCKD-controlled labyrinth that may turn out to be the deadliest maze of all. Anyone who makes it out alive will get answers to the questions that the Gladers have been asking since they arrived in the maze.

programme.title: War for the Planet of the Apes
programme.desc: Caesar (Andy Serkis) and his apes are forced into a deadly conflict with an army of humans led by a ruthless colonel (Woody Harrelson). After the apes suffer unimaginable losses, Caesar wrestles with his darker instincts and begins his own mythic quest to avenge his kind. As the journey finally brings them face to face, Caesar and the colonel are pitted against each other in an epic battle that will determine the fate of both of their species and the future of the planet.

programme.title: The Alto Knights
programme.desc: In the 1950s, notorious New York crime bosses Frank Costello and Vito Genovese vie for control of the city streets. Once the best of friends, petty jealousies and a series of betrayals place them on a deadly collision course that reshapes organized crime forever.

programme.title: Colette
programme.desc: After moving to Paris, author Sidonie-Gabrielle Colette agrees to ghostwrite a semi-autobiographical novel for her husband. Its success soon inspires her to fight for creative ownership and overcome the societal constraints of the early 20th century.

programme.title: Broken City
programme.desc: Former cop Billy Taggart (Mark Wahlberg) sees a chance at redemption for past sins when New York City's Mayor Nicolas Hostetler (Russell Crowe) calls on him for a special job. Hostetler thinks his wife, Cathleen (Catherine Zeta-Jones), is having an affair and that it may hurt his chances for re-election. However, a bigger scandal than expected pulls in Billy right at the start of his investigation. Double-crossed by the mayor, Billy begins a relentless quest for justice.

programme.title: Kiss of the Spider Woman
programme.desc: In a prison cell somewhere in Latin America, two very different men warily confront each other. Molina (William Hurt) is first seen wrapping his head in a towel, in the shape of a turban, while Valentin (Raul Julia), bearded and classically macho in appearance, watches with a mixture of fascination and revulsion. During the time spent together, the two men come to understand and respect each other.

programme.title: Marie Antoinette
programme.desc: An Austrian teenager (Kirsten Dunst) marries the Dauphin (Jason Schwartzman) of France and becomes that country's queen following the death of King Louis XV (Judy Davis) in 1774. Years later, after a life of luxury and privilege, Marie Antoinette loses her head during the French Revolution.

programme.title: Opus
programme.desc: A journalist is invited to the remote compound of a legendary pop star who mysteriously disappeared 30 years ago. Surrounded by the star's cult of sycophants, she gets caught in the middle of his twisted plan.

programme.title: The Watch
programme.desc: In Glenview, Ohio, four pals, Evan, Bob, Franklin and Jamarcus, form a neighborhood watch group. Armed with flashlights, walkie-talkies and spiffy new jackets, the guys try to take their mission seriously, even if they spend a little time goofing off now and then. But when they discover aliens living among them and no one believes their story, they learn that they have to kick things up a notch to save the world from annihilation.

programme.title: A Hard Day's Night
programme.desc: The Beatles in their feature film debut, one of the greatest rock-and-roll comedy adventures ever. The film has a fully restored negative and digitally restored soundtrack. The film takes on the just-left-of-reality style of mock-documentary, following "a day in the life" of John, Paul, George, and Ringo as fame takes them by storm.

programme.title: Gimme Shelter
programme.desc: The landmark documentary about the tragically ill-fated Rolling Stones free concert at Altamont Speedway on December 6, 1969. Only four months earlier, Woodstock defined the Love Generation; now it lay in ruins on a desolate racetrack six miles outside of San Francisco.

programme.title: Brian Banks
programme.desc: The inspirational true story of Brian Banks, an all-American high school football star who finds his life upended when he's wrongly convicted of a crime he didn't commit. Despite the lack of evidence, Banks gets railroaded through a broken justice system and sentenced to a decade of prison and probation. Years later, with the support of Justin Brooks and the California Innocence Project, Banks fights to reclaim his life and fulfill his dreams of playing in the NFL.

programme.title: Love, Simon
programme.desc: Everyone deserves a great love story, but for 17-year-old Simon Spier, it's a little more complicated. He hasn't told his family or friends that he's gay, and he doesn't know the identity of the anonymous classmate that he's fallen for online. Resolving both issues proves hilarious, terrifying and life-changing.

programme.title: Love & Mercy
programme.desc: In the late 1960s, the Beach Boys' Brian Wilson stops touring, produces "Pet Sounds" and begins to lose his grip on reality. By the 1980s, Wilson, under the sway of a controlling therapist, finds a savior in Melinda Ledbetter.

programme.title: Sound of My Voice
programme.desc: California couple Peter (Christopher Denham) and Lorna (Nicole Vicius) set out to make a documentary meant to expose a charismatic cult leader named Maggie (Brit Marling) as a fraud. Maggie claims to be from the year 2054 and is seemingly allergic to almost everything in her environment; she lives in a basement, subsisting on canned oxygen, homegrown food and the blood of her acolytes. Though at first, Peter and Lorna are adamant that Maggie is a fake, they both soon fall under her spell.

programme.title: The Three Stooges
programme.desc: Left on the doorstep of an orphanage run by nuns, three newborn knuckleheads grow up to be finger-poking, nyuk-nyuking janitors named Larry (Sean Hayes), Curly (Will Sasso) and Moe (Chris Diamantopoulos). When they learn that financial problems will soon force the only home they've ever known to close, the trio sets out to raise $830,000 in one month. Out in the world for the very first time, the three innocent bumblers become embroiled in a murder plot and find stardom on a TV reality show.

programme.title: Waitress: The Musical
programme.desc: Jenna Hunterson is a waitress and expert pie maker stuck in a small town and a loveless marriage. When a baking contest offers her a chance at escape, Jenna fights to reclaim part of herself.

programme.title: Laughter Chefs Unlimited Entertainment S01
programme.desc: A mouthwatering mix of celebrity charm, culinary prowess, and comedic flair! Six dynamic couples bring laughter and flavor to the kitchen stage in this delightful non-fiction extravaganza.

programme.title: Tuu Juliet Jatt Di
programme.desc: Nawab feels uneasy and regrets his night with Tina. Buzzo advises him to keep it hidden from Heer and find a way to handle the complicated situation with Tina.

programme.title: Tuu Juliet Jatt Di
programme.desc: Another portrayal of the same heartfelt love journey, highlighting charm, humor, and the small emotional conflicts that slowly build into meaningful relationships.

programme.title: Tuu Juliet Jatt Di
programme.desc: Another portrayal of the same heartfelt love journey, highlighting charm, humor, and the small emotional conflicts that slowly build into meaningful relationships.

programme.title: The Adventures Of Srimannarayana
programme.desc: A witty, fearless cop arrives in a quirky town to solve a treasure mystery. His investigation takes him through hilarious encounters, thrilling chases, and clever mind games with eccentric locals.

programme.title: Dove
programme.desc: A regular boy loves a wealthy girl, but his life shifts when he's jailed for murder.

programme.title: Prema Katha Chitram 2
programme.desc: Sudheer faces supernatural turmoil when Bindu's vengeful spirit possesses his new love interest, Nandu.

programme.desc: (2017) Hugh Jackman, Zac Efron and Michelle Williams star in the Golden-Globe-winning, toe-tapping smash hit musical about the life and times of P.T. Barnum, who created the circus.

programme.desc: (2011) Comedy with Simon Bird, Joe Thomas, Blake Harrison and James Buckley. Sixth form's over, so the lads go on a rowdy holiday to Crete, with lots of sun, sea and sex in mind...

programme.desc: (1993) Richard Linklater's school's-out-forever comedy-drama with Ben Affleck, Milla Jovovich and Matthew McConaughey. Texan students let loose to celebrate their freedom in the summer of 1976.

programme.title: The Worst Person in the World
programme.desc: (2021) Award-winning drama. Julie's about to turn 30. She did well at school. But what does she want now, in work and in love? And will her search for perfection see her miss out? In Norwegian/subs.

programme.desc: (2014) Comedy with Simon Bird, Joe Thomas, James Buckley and Blake Harrison. Simon, Will and Neil dash to Australia when Jay tells them that he's living a life of hedonism as a star DJ in Sydney.

programme.title: Jackass Forever
programme.desc: (2022) Raucous comedy with Johnny Knoxville, Wee Man and friends. The Jackasses reunite for more painfully real stunts. Older? Yes. Wiser? You decide.

programme.title: Scream
programme.desc: (2022) Meta-comedy horror 're-quel' with Courteney Cox. Killer Ghostface returns to shock the younger generation out of their love of 'elevated horror' with a bit of old-fashioned slashing. [AD,S]
